title: Learning JavaScript Patterns for Scalability
excerpt: Diving deep into design patterns and how they help build better web applications.
content:
As applications grow from simple scripts to complex systems, the architecture becomes as important as the logic. Design patterns are proven solutions to common problems in software design.

Why Patterns Matter

They provide a common language for developers and help prevent code from becoming 'spaghetti'. In modern JavaScript, we often see patterns like Module, Observer, and Singleton being used to manage state and organize logic.

// Example of a simple Singleton
const Database = (function() {
  let instance;
  function createInstance() {
    return new Object("I am the instance");
  }
  return {
    getInstance: function() {
      if (!instance) instance = createInstance();
      return instance;
    }
  };
})();

Understanding these patterns allows you to build systems that are not only functional but also maintainable and scalable.
